name: Squid Salmple
description: Eurorack 8-channel mono sampler. Each channel has ~11s 16-bit/44.1kHz RAM, per-channel bit/rate/speed/pitch, multimode filter, A/D envelope, looping with crossfade, multiple cue sets. Channels 6-8 have 1V/oct.

controls.name: Chan
controls.description: Button cycles to next channel. Hold + twist encoder scrolls channels on the current parameter page.
controls.detail: Hold + Func: swap two channels · Hold + Rec: lock record destination

controls.name: Program Knob
controls.description: Push-turn encoder for all UI. Twist adjusts values, click plays current channel's sample or confirms menu action.
controls.detail: Hold Func + turn: fine adjust or zoom in Cues · Click from record screen: preview

controls.name: USB Button
controls.description: Opens USB screen for bank/set/pool loading, saving, renaming. Also goes back up menus.
controls.detail: Scroll left of Bank 1: access Sets · scroll further: Pool

controls.name: Quality
controls.description: Cycles pages: Bits, Rate, Speed, Pitch (or Quant on ch 6-8), Filter type, Frequency, Resonance.
controls.detail: Func + Quality: reassign channel output to neighbour pair

controls.name: Env
controls.description: Cycles pages: Level, Decay, Attack, E Trig (EOS destination), Steps (round-robin triggering).
controls.detail: Func + Env: mute channel · Chan + Func + Env: solo

controls.name: Loop
controls.description: Cycles pages: Loop mode (forward / loop / zig-zag / gate), X-Fade crossfade length.
controls.detail: Gate mode: sample plays only while trigger held (like sustain)

controls.name: Cues
controls.description: Cycles Start, End, Loop cue points. Twist moves cue, Func+twist zooms around current cue.
controls.detail: Func + Cues: access Cue Sets menu (Add / Split / Auto / RM ALL)

controls.name: Reverse
controls.detail: Hold Reverse + Assign button: assign CV to Reverse

controls.name: Func
controls.description: Modifier for secondary functions (fine adjust, zoom, mute, solo, etc).
controls.detail: Func + Rec: toggle record monitor · Func + Cues: Cue Sets

controls.name: CV Assign 1 / 2 / 3
controls.description: Three buttons — on any parameter page, press an Assign button to map that parameter to CV input 1, 2, or 3. Press again to unassign.
controls.detail: Func + Assign: edit attenuation/offset for that CV pair

controls.name: V/Oct (×3)
controls.description: Dedicated pitch knob per channel 6/7/8. Sums with V/oct jack.
controls.detail: tuning-fork icon shows on screen when near original sample pitch · ~2-3 octaves useful range

controls.name: Level
controls.description: Input gain for audio/CV being sampled. Red LED lights at clipping.
controls.detail: modular level to line level · CV sampling tricky: keep low

controls.name: Rec
controls.description: Arms and starts recording. First press arms, second press records, third press stops.
controls.detail: Modes cycled by encoder while armed: Replace · Overdub (-3dB duck) · Extend

inputs.name: Trigger Inputs 1-8
inputs.description: Per-channel trigger jacks. Trigger starts playback of that channel's sample.
inputs.voltage: trigger/gate · gate with Loop=GATE mode sustains playback

inputs.name: Chan
inputs.description: Trigger steps active channel. Firmware 185+ reconfigurable: Choke all / Punch-in cue / Load next bank / Reset cue steps / Reset step triggers / Toggle monitor.

inputs.name: V/Oct (CH 6 / 7 / 8)
inputs.description: 1V/oct pitch input per dedicated channel. Expect crunchy lo-fi aliasing — not a clean VCO.

inputs.name: Freq
inputs.description: Attenuverted CV input for sample playback frequency. Routes after the V/oct.

inputs.name: CV 1 / 2 / 3
inputs.description: Three assignable CV inputs. Any parameter (except Steps) can be routed with per-assignment attenuation and offset.

inputs.name: Signal
inputs.description: Mono record input. Accepts modular to line level audio or CV.
inputs.voltage: audio/CV · level trim via Level knob

inputs.name: Rec
inputs.description: Automated record trigger. <500ms trigger enables envelope-follower mode (records until source drops to silence); longer = gate (records while high).

outputs.name: Trigger
outputs.description: Outputs +5V gate while recording is active, plus EOS triggers from channels with E Trig set to ON. Useful for triggering drum modules during sample-then-replay automation.

outputs.name: 1+2 Out
outputs.description: DC-coupled sum output for channels 1 and 2 (assignable to 3+4 via Func+Quality). Patching here removes those channels from Mix Out.

outputs.name: 3+4 Out
outputs.description: DC-coupled sum for channels 3-4 (assignable). Patching removes from Mix Out.

outputs.name: 5+6 Out
outputs.description: DC-coupled sum for channels 5-6 (assignable to 7+8). Patching removes from Mix Out.

outputs.name: 7+8 Out
outputs.description: DC-coupled sum for channels 7-8 (assignable). Patching removes from Mix Out.

outputs.name: Mix Out
outputs.description: AC-coupled full mix of all 8 channels (minus any patched into pair outs). Blocks DC — will not reproduce recorded CV/envelopes.

behaviors.name: Per-channel sample mangling
behaviors.description: Each channel has independent bit depth, sample rate, playback speed, pitch (chans 1-5) or quantised V/oct (chans 6-8), multimode filter (LP/BP/Notch/HP), frequency, and resonance. Filter is CPU-heavy — enabling many at once can lag the UI and introduce stutters, especially combined with pitch shifting.

behaviors.name: Speed vs Pitch (timestretch)
behaviors.description: Speed is linear playback speed; Pitch shifts pitch without changing length. Combining the two produces timestretch-like effects. Chans 6-8 replace these with V/oct quantised playback for musical tracking — at cost of losing Speed/Pitch independence.

behaviors.name: Loop modes (forward / zig-zag / gate)
behaviors.description: Single arrow: forward playback. Arrow + loop symbol: loops between loop and end points. Bidirectional arrows: zig-zag (ping-pong between loop and end). GATE: plays and loops only while the channel's trigger input is held high (sustain). X-Fade smooths loop boundaries.

behaviors.name: Cue sets for beat slicing
behaviors.description: Each channel holds multiple sets of Start/End/Loop points — switching sets reshapes playback without loading a new sample. Auto detects transients and zero crossings to create cue sets from a break beat automatically. Split divides existing sets into equal halves. CV can select active cue set across the 0-5V range. WAV file markers auto-import as cue sets.

behaviors.name: Choke groups
behaviors.description: Assign a channel to choke another — triggering the owner stops the chokee mid-playback. Display shows 'Cx' on the channel. Classic hi-hat style mutual exclusion, or use for vocal/sample truncation effects. Chan input alt-mode can also choke all channels at once.

behaviors.name: E Trig (end-of-sample trigger)
behaviors.description: Fires a trigger when a sample finishes playing. Can target another channel internally (chains sample-after-sample sequences without external clocking) or bus to the physical Trigger output. Combine with different end points per channel to generate percussion phrases where each hit cues the next.

behaviors.name: Step triggering (polyphonic round-robin)
behaviors.description: Cycles incoming triggers across N adjacent channels in round-robin fashion — physical triggers only, encoder clicks don't cycle. Lets one trigger input drive polyphonic voice allocation across channels with different samples or settings. Steps cannot be CV-assigned.

behaviors.name: Cross-channel sample reference
behaviors.description: A channel can reference another channel's sample data rather than its own, enabling two channels to play the same source at different speeds/pitches/cue points — huge for delay/dub effects or duplicating breaks for polyrhythmic slicing.

behaviors.name: Record modes: Replace / Overdub / Extend
behaviors.description: Replace overwrites (default, clears cue sets). Overdub layers new audio atop existing at -3dB, preserving cue sets. Extend appends to the end of existing audio and automatically adds a new cue set marking the addition — lets you build 8-slice loops by successive short recordings into one channel.

behaviors.name: Envelope-follower vs gate recording
behaviors.description: A trigger <500ms into Rec starts recording and auto-stops when the input signal decays to silence (envelope-follower mode) — perfect for capturing one-shot hits. Longer triggers are treated as gates: recording runs while the gate is high. Max sample length 11s per channel; recording past that wraps to beginning.

behaviors.name: CV as audio (DC-coupled pair outs)
behaviors.description: Pair outputs are DC-coupled — they pass envelopes and CV sampled from modular sources. Mix Out is AC-coupled and strips DC, so recorded CV must be tapped from the pair outs to function as CV. Enables using the Squid as a sample-and-playback envelope or LFO recorder.

behaviors.name: Output pair reassignment
behaviors.description: Channels 1-4 can be routed to either the 1+2 out or the 3+4 out; channels 5-8 to the 5+6 or 7+8 out. Lets you isolate any channel to its own jack for external processing while keeping others summed.

behaviors.name: Reverse CV with special assignment
behaviors.description: Reverse gets a different CV assignment flow than other parameters — hold the Reverse button while pressing an Assign button to map a CV input to forward/reverse state. Lets a gate or LFO flip playback direction mid-sample for glitch effects.

patchIdeas.name: Auto-sliced break beat
patchIdeas.patch: Record a breakbeat to Ch 1 · Func+Cues → Auto (transient detection) generates cue sets per hit · CV Assign 1 to active cue set · random or stepped CV into CV 1 sweeps through slices on each trigger · drums chopped without a computer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Breakbeat loaded · Cue Sets: Auto
* CV 1 → Active Cue Set

Clock:

Random:

- Clock (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 1) // fire slice
- Random (Out) >> Squid Salmple (CV 1) // pick slice
- Squid Salmple (1+2 Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Clocked single-channel delay
patchIdeas.patch: Ch 1 = Rec destination (Chan+Rec locks it) · Ch 2 references Ch 1's buffer (Chan+turn) · clock /4 → Trigger 2 for playback · Rec input receives slow clock · live audio loops through record buffer with pitch-shifted replay · external filter for feedback and darkening
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Rec locked · Extend mode
* CH 2: references CH 1 · Speed ~0.9

Clock:

Filter:

- Audio Src (Out) -> Squid Salmple (Signal)
- Clock (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Rec) // timed sampling
- Clock (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 2) // playback tap
- Squid Salmple (1+2 Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Mixer (In) // feedback darkening
- Mixer (Out) -> Squid Salmple (Signal) // wet-dry return

patchIdeas.name: Single-cycle wavetable VCO
patchIdeas.patch: Load or record a rich tone to Ch 7 · Loop ON · zoom Cues (Func+turn) and align Start/End to zero crossings for a single cycle · Quant=OT (one per octave) · V/oct sequencer → CH 7 V/oct · sample now plays as a pitched oscillator with crunchy lo-fi character
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 7: Loop forward · Start/End: 1 cycle · Quant=OT
* CH 7 V/oct knob: calibrate to fork icon

Sequencer:

VCF:

VCA:

- Sequencer (V/oct) p> Squid Salmple (V/oct CH 7)
- Sequencer (Gate) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 7)
- Squid Salmple (7+8 Out) -> VCF (In) // clean up aliasing
- VCF (Out) -> VCA (In)

patchIdeas.name: End-of-sample percussion chain
patchIdeas.patch: Ch 1 kick samples · Ch 1 E Trig → CH 2 (E Trig Env page) · Ch 2 snare fires the moment Ch 1 finishes · Ch 2 E Trig → CH 3 hi-hat · each end cues the next step of a percussion phrase with no external sequencer
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Kick · E Trig target=2
* CH 2: Snare · E Trig target=3
* CH 3: Hi-hat

Clock:

- Clock (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 1) // starts chain
- Squid Salmple (Mix Out) -> Output (In) // internal E Trigs chain 1→2→3

patchIdeas.name: CV envelope recorder and looper
patchIdeas.patch: Patch MATHS rise/fall envelope to Signal · low input level · Rec as envelope-follower · record the envelope to Ch 8 · Loop ON · pair output 7+8 (DC-coupled) carries the envelope as CV · use it to modulate another VCF · lo-fi stepped envelope recall
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 8: Loop ON · recorded envelope
* Mode: Rec envelope-follower

MATHS:

VCF:

- MATHS (CH 1 OUT) >> Squid Salmple (Signal) // record CV as audio
- Clock Source (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 8) // replay trigger
- Squid Salmple (7+8 Out) >> VCF (Cutoff) // DC-coupled CV replay

patchIdeas.name: Polyphonic round-robin voice
patchIdeas.patch: Load 4 variations of the same snare to CH 1-4 · CH 1 Env → Steps=4 · single trigger into Trigger 1 round-robins through CH 1/2/3/4 · four slightly different snare samples alternate every hit · instant sample variation for humanised drums
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Snare v1 · Steps=4 (round-robin CH 1-4)
* CH 2-4: Snare v2/v3/v4

Drum Trigger:

- Drum Trigger (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 1)
- Squid Salmple (Mix Out) -> Output (In) // rotates through snares

patchIdeas.name: Gate-sustained granular drone
patchIdeas.patch: Load texture/pad to CH 1 · Loop mode = GATE (plays/loops only while trigger held) · Ch 1 loop points tight (50-200ms window) · Bits lowered to 8 · Filter LP enabled · long gate → Trigger 1 sustains a lo-fi granular drone from that tiny window
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Loop=GATE · tight loop window · Bits=8 · Filter=LP

Gate Source:

- Gate Source (Out) g> Squid Salmple (Trigger 1) // sustain
- Squid Salmple (1+2 Out) -> Reverb (In) // granular pad

patchIdeas.name: Choke group hi-hat pair
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 closed hat · CH 2 open hat · CH 2 Choke=1 (open chokes on closed hit) · Pam or clock triggers → Trigger 1 and Trigger 2 · classic drum machine behaviour where a closed hat kills any decaying open hat
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Closed hat
* CH 2: Open hat, Choke=1

Clock:

- Clock (OUT 1) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 1) // closed on
- Clock (OUT 2) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 2) // open on off-beats
- Squid Salmple (Mix Out) -> Output (In) // closed kills open

patchIdeas.name: Reverse glitch stutter
patchIdeas.patch: CH 1 long vocal sample · Reverse assigned to CV 1 (hold Reverse + Assign 1) · square LFO → CV 1 · playback flips forward/reverse at LFO rate · combine with short loop for stuttering glitch texture · excellent over breaks or pads
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Vocal · Loop=zig-zag · Reverse→CV 1

LFO:

Clock:

- LFO (Square) >> Squid Salmple (CV 1) // flip direction
- Clock (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 1)
- Squid Salmple (1+2 Out) -> Delay (In) // glitch chop

patchIdeas.name: Live-resampling feedback loop
patchIdeas.patch: External synth → Signal · Chan+Rec locks CH 1 as rec destination · Mix Out → external FX → back into Signal · Extend mode appends each new record cycle · each pass adds layer and FX processing · classic tape-style infinite accumulation
patchIdeas.diagram:
Squid Salmple:
* CH 1: Rec locked · Extend mode · Loop ON

VCO:

FX:

- VCO (Out) -> FX (In)
- FX (Out) -> Squid Salmple (Signal) // live resample
- Clock Source (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Rec) // record cycles
- Clock Source (Out) t> Squid Salmple (Trigger 1)
- Squid Salmple (Mix Out) -> FX (In) // feedback via FX
